prompt:
Use case: style-transfer
Asset type: runtime tactics unit sprite
Primary request: derive a Children of Prophecy Mirage Seer from the original faction concept and the approved Aevum Guardian anchor while keeping the same faction identity
Subject: a psychic controller from Children of Prophecy with layered veil wrappings, wide sleeves, a ritual focus or suspended charm bundle, and a calm but uncanny battlefield stance; lighter and more mystical than Aevum Guardian without becoming a floating wizard
Style/medium: moody post-apocalyptic tactics sprite illustration with painterly pixel-art-inspired rendering, strong readable silhouette, larger shape language, and restrained detail for clean downscaling
Composition/framing: single full-body subject, centered, grounded stance, transparent background, tight sprite bounds, readable when rendered around 100 to 110 px tall on a tactics board
Lighting/mood: solemn desert dusk light with restrained psychic shimmer and controlled contrast, spiritual but not flashy
Color palette: sun-bleached ivory cloth, ash beige, muted rust red, worn brown leather, faint pale cyan or ghostly teal accents used sparingly
Constraints: preserve the Children of Prophecy silhouette language from the faction concept and approved anchor, keep the conical-hat and wrapped-face lineage readable without copying the Aevum Guardian pose, exactly one subject, no floor, no scenery, no text, no extra subjects, no oversized empty padding
Avoid: neon magic, anime rendering, glossy sci-fi materials, floating levitation pose, giant VFX, cinematic background, noisy micro-detail, ornate fantasy robes

Use case: style-transfer
Asset type: runtime tactics unit sprite
Primary request: derive a Children of Prophecy Memory Keeper from the original faction concept and the approved Aevum Guardian anchor while keeping the same faction identity
Subject: a contemplative support operative from Children of Prophecy with relic satchels, wrapped robes, a staff or focus bundle, and a disciplined pilgrim-healer stance; still grounded and combat-ready rather than ceremonial only
Style/medium: moody post-apocalyptic tactics sprite illustration with painterly pixel-art-inspired rendering, strong readable silhouette, larger shape language, and restrained detail for clean downscaling
Composition/framing: single full-body subject, centered, grounded stance, transparent background, tight sprite bounds, readable when rendered around 100 to 110 px tall on a tactics board
Lighting/mood: warm dust-filtered dusk light with subtle sacred-tech glow kept low and practical
Color palette: sun-bleached ivory cloth, weathered sand tones, muted rust red, dark leather, restrained pale gold and faint teal accents
Constraints: preserve the Children of Prophecy lineage from the faction concept and approved anchor, keep the conical-hat or wrapped-head silhouette family readable while making this unit clearly support-oriented and distinct from Aevum Guardian, exactly one subject, no floor, no scenery, no text, no extra subjects, no oversized empty padding
Avoid: fantasy priest cliches, giant halo effects, neon magic, anime rendering, glossy sci-fi materials, floating pose, cinematic background, noisy micro-detail
